forecasts.reasoning: The most recent change to the Dow Jones Industrial Average was on November 8, 2024, when Nvidia and Sherwin-Williams replaced Intel and Dow Inc. Multiple searches for news in 2025 regarding components being added or removed yielded no results. A Wikipedia snippet from March 2026 definitively confirms that "As of November 8, 2024, the components of the DJIA have changed 59 times," implying no subsequent changes or announcements occurred before July 1, 2025. Because index component changes are rare and there is strong evidence no changes occurred in the relevant timeframe, the probability of any listed company being dropped before July 1, 2025, is extremely low.

Although President Trump announced his intention to revoke Harvard's tax-exempt status on May 2, 2025, revoking a 501(c)(3) designation requires a lengthy administrative and legal procedure. Various contemporary articles from subsequent months indicate that standard IRS processes and rule-makings had just begun to be considered by June 2025. Furthermore, articles written later in 2025 (e.g., in August and November) discuss the ongoing 'battle' over the status, Harvard's responsive lawsuits, and the potential recourses an institution might have *if* its status is revoked, rather than discussing an actual finalized revocation. This reporting confirms that while the threat was imminent, the actual revocation did not legally materialize prior to the July 1, 2025 deadline.

Extensive searches for coups in Africa and Latin America in 2024 and 2025 reveal no clear successful coups before the July 1, 2025 deadline. In Africa, year-end roundups for 2025 identify exactly two successful coups (Madagascar in October and Guinea-Bissau in November) and one failed attempt (Chad in January). Since the successful African coups occurred after July 1, 2025, they do not trigger a positive resolution. We also found failed attempts in DRC (May 2024) and Benin (Dec 2024). In Latin America, the only major military action was the failed attempt in Bolivia (June 2024). Haiti’s government transition in early 2024 was driven by gang violence, which does not meet the criteria of a coup d'état by state apparatus. Finally, while Nicolás Maduro refused to relinquish power after the July 2024 Venezuelan election, this is overwhelmingly characterized as electoral fraud in mainstream media rather than a formal self-coup, and he arguably fails the requirement of having "come to power through legal means" given his disputed 2018 election. Thus, the condition of a successful coup before July 1, 2025 remains unmet.
